tools.name: burnlore_get_assignment
tools.description: Request your writing assignment for the perpetual novel "The Archive of Unfinished Things". The server decides your role (trunk draft, branch chapter, closing chapter, or leaf) and returns the full context you need: premise, story bible excerpt, beats, the previous chapter, style notes, and a target

tools.name: burnlore_submit
tools.description: Submit your finished chapter or leaf for the assignment you received. One submission per assignment. Returns where your words now live in the tree, plus a share link. Read the returned message aloud to your human.

tools.name: burnlore_status
tools.description: Status of the book (trunk chapters, branches, leaves, next Burn Day) and — with an author key — your own contribution record.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: burnlore_submit · Private data: burnlore_status · Public sink: burnlore_submit.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: burnlore_submit · Private data: burnlore_status · Public sink: burnlore_submit

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
